The visual state of components (which section is selected, what text is in the search input, and which FAQs are displayed) is always derived from the combination of URL parameters and the latest getFaqSections/getFaqItems responses.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial page load, reads URL parameters, triggers data loading, and manages top-level state for the FAQ page.
Displays the list of FAQ sections returned by getFaqSections and allows the user to filter FAQs by selecting a section.
data_binding: activeSectionId // derived from URL sectionId parameter

components.functionality: Allows users to enter a keyword or phrase to search FAQs via getFaqItems, and keeps the query synchronized with the URL.
data_binding: searchQuery // derived from URL query parameter

components.functionality: Summarizes the current FAQ filters (active section and search query) and the number of results based on the latest getFaqItems response; provides controls to clear filters.
Renders the collection of FAQ items returned by getFaqItems as a list; delegates expand/collapse behavior and navigation actions to child components.
data_binding: getFaqItems(response).[] // each item {id, question, answer, sectionId, sectionName}

components.functionality: For each FAQ item, manages the expanded/collapsed state and displays the question and answer. Optionally displays context-specific navigation actions based on the item's section.

Determines and renders navigation links from a given FAQ item to related detail pages (e.g., tickets.html, getting_here.html, accessibility.html, groups.html) based on the FAQ item's sectionId or sectionName.
data_binding: sectionToPageMapping // configuration mapping sections to outgoing page URLs

components.functionality: Displays when getFaqItems returns an empty array for the current filters, and offers actions to adjust or clear filters.
Keeps internal state variables (activeSectionId, searchQuery) synchronized with the URL parameters and triggers getFaqItems whenever relevant URL parameters change.
Abstracts calls to getFaqSections and getFaqItems; ensures that getFaqItems is always called with the current sectionId and query derived from state or URL.

layout.grid_system: Likely a 12-column responsive grid with a centered max-width content container (~1100–1200px). The hero spans full-width, but inner content appears constrained within a central column.
layout.layout_pattern: The page keeps the mockup’s structure: a full-width, red bokeh hero at the top with overlaid global navigation and a large centered page title ('Frequently Asked Questions'). Below the hero, content sits on a white background inside a centered, max-width container. Within this container, a two-column layout emerges on desktop: the left, wider column (about 60–65% of the container width) holds all functional FAQ controls and results (filters, search, summary, list/empty state). The right, narrower column (about 35–40%) is reserved for the FAQ-related links panel, echoing the mockup’s tall image column as a secondary, supportive element. Non-visual components (controller, loader, URL sync) are layered logically but don’t affect the structural flow.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page keeps the mockup’s structure: a full-width, red bokeh hero at the top with overlaid global navigation and a large centered page title ('Frequently Asked Questions'). Below the hero, content sits on a white background inside a centered, max-width container. Within this container, a two-column layout emerges on desktop: the left, wider column (about 60–65% of the container width) holds all functional FAQ controls and results (filters, search, summary, list/empty state). The right, narrower column (about 35–40%) is reserved for the FAQ-related links panel, echoing the mockup’s tall image column as a secondary, supportive element. Non-visual components (controller, loader, URL sync) are layered logically but don’t affect the structural flow.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The hero occupies the top ~35–40% of the initial viewport height, with generous vertical padding around the FAQ title. Navigation is a slim bar at the very top, floating above the hero background. The main content container below uses large left/right margins to keep content around 1100–1200px wide. Vertical spacing between functional blocks in the left column (filters, search, summary, list) follows the 24–48px rhythm: about 24px between tightly related elements (search and summary) and 32–48px between larger functional blocks (filters vs. results list). The right column’s related links card has comfortable internal padding (24–32px) and top alignment with the primary controls. On mobile, the two columns collapse into a single column with components stacked in a clear logical order.
layout_strategies.content_density: Content density stays low-to-medium, optimized for scannability and reading, similar to an editorial layout. Each FAQ accordion item uses generous vertical padding and clear separation (16–24px between items) to avoid crowding. Filters and search are grouped but not cramped; there is visible breathing room above and below them. The related links panel is visually light with ample whitespace around link groups. Overall, the design avoids packing too many controls into a single horizontal row; instead, elements are stacked with consistent vertical rhythm.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: Visual flow mirrors the mockup’s Z-pattern. On page load, the eye goes to the top-left logo, then right across the navigation, then settles on the large centered 'Frequently Asked Questions' hero heading. Scrolling down, the eye is drawn to the left edge of the white content area where the filter controls appear first, then down to the search field, then the results summary, and into the list of FAQ accordions. The right column’s related links attract secondary attention once the user begins exploring the FAQs, acting as a contextual sidebar. The loader and empty state are positioned within the same left-column region so they replace the list content without disrupting the established flow.

layout.overall_description: The FAQ page opens with a dramatic, full-width red bokeh hero background, overlaid by a slim global navigation bar at the very top and a large, centered 'Frequently Asked Questions' title with generous top and bottom padding, giving it strong presence against the festive backdrop. As the user scrolls past the hero, the background switches to white and a centered content container appears, capped at around 1100–1200px wide with ample whitespace on either side. Inside this container, the layout splits into two columns on desktop: on the left, a wider column that vertically stacks the FAQ filters, a roomy search bar, a compact results summary, and then the FAQ list itself, rendered as a vertical stack of accordion items with comfortable separation. On the right, a narrower column houses a tall, card-like related links panel aligned to the top of the filters, echoing the mockup’s tall image column as a secondary visual anchor. When there are no results, a generous empty state appears where the list would be, centered in the left column. Invisible infrastructure components manage loading and URL syncing without altering the visual layout, apart from a subtle loading treatment centered over the list area. The overall effect is a warm, seasonal, editorial-style FAQ experience that feels spacious, organized, and easy to scan.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual, top-level controller that conceptually sits behind the entire page layout. It doesn’t occupy its own visible space but governs the initialization of the hero, main content container, and all child components. In terms of spatial behavior, it is associated with the full viewport, from the top of the hero to the bottom of the page, ensuring that filters, search, results, and related links render within the defined two-column grid inside the centered content container.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, full-page controller; no direct footprint in the layout but logically wraps all visible components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: In the main white content section directly beneath the hero, the FAQ filter control is the first interactive element in the left column. It aligns flush with the left edge of the main content container (respecting the inset grid) and extends across roughly 80–90% of the left column’s width, leaving a small margin on the right for breathing room. Vertically, it sits about 48px below the bottom edge of the hero section to clearly separate hero and content. If filters are displayed as pills or dropdowns, they appear in a single row or two staggered rows with 8–16px gaps, but the overall filter area keeps about 16–24px of internal padding above and below. On smaller screens, this filter block becomes full-width, centered within the single-column flow with similar top/bottom spacing.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows with the page scroll; anchored at the top of the left column. On smaller screens, it expands to full-width and stacks above the search form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The search form is placed immediately below the filter controls within the left column, separated by a modest vertical gap of about 16–24px. It is visually prominent: a full-width search input (relative to the left column) spanning about 90–100% of that column’s width, centered horizontally within it. The input field is tall and airy, with 12–16px internal padding and clear left/right margins so it doesn’t feel cramped against the column boundaries. Any search button or icon is aligned to the right inside the field or just to its right, but the entire search area is visually grouped as a single block. On mobile, the search form appears directly under the filters, still full-width, maintaining similar spacing above and below to preserve scannability.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline block in the left column that scrolls with the page; adjusts responsively to occupy the full width of the single-column layout on smaller viewports.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The results summary appears as a compact text row directly beneath the search form, aligned to the left edge of the search field. It is separated from the search form by about 8–16px of vertical space, signaling its tight relationship to the search without crowding. The summary spans roughly 60–70% of the left column width, leaving open white space to the right for visual lightness. It uses subtle typography—smaller, possibly lighter-weight text—so it doesn’t compete with headings or button labels. On mobile, it remains immediately below the search field, left-aligned within the single-column layout, with the same modest gap.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, left-aligned text element that scrolls with the content; no special sticky or overlay behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The FAQ list occupies the main vertical space in the left column, starting about 24–32px below the results summary to create a clear separation between controls and content. It extends down the page as needed, aligned with the left and right boundaries of the left column (about 60–65% of the main container width). Each FAQ item appears as a full-width row within this column, and the list as a whole creates a strong vertical reading line from top to bottom. Between individual items, there is 16–24px of vertical spacing to keep them distinct. As the user scrolls, the list continues down while the right column sidebar remains visible where possible. On small screens, the list becomes full width, sitting directly under the results summary, occupying the entire content area below the fold.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Scrollable vertical stack within the left column, extending as long as necessary; no fixed height, adapts to content length.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each FAQ item accordion is a full-width card-like row within the FAQ list, spanning the width of the left column. The question header bar is left-aligned, with the question text constrained to a comfortable reading width (roughly 55–70 characters) and generous vertical padding of 12–16px above and below. An expand/collapse icon is right-aligned within the same row, maintaining a small right margin from the column edge. When expanded, the answer content slides down directly beneath the question, indented slightly to align with the question text, and uses 12–16px of internal padding and 16–24px of spacing before the next accordion begins. Visually, each accordion is separated from the next by a subtle divider or spacing, maintaining the low-to-medium density feel. On mobile, the accordion remains full-width, with the same padding scaled appropriately, and the expand/collapse interactions maintain clear separation between items.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Expands and collapses vertically within the list, pushing subsequent items down; no overlapping or floating content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The related links component occupies the right-hand column of the main content section, mirroring the vertical orientation of the historical image in the mockup. It aligns to the top of the left column’s filter/search area, creating a balanced horizontal row at the top of the content section. The component spans roughly 35–40% of the overall content container width and is inset slightly from the container’s right edge to maintain consistent side margins. Inside, links are grouped in a tall card with 24–32px of padding on all sides, and link groups are separated by 16–24px of vertical space to keep the area airy and scannable. This column extends vertically as needed but typically remains shorter than the full FAQ list, serving as a secondary visual anchor. On scroll, on larger screens, the related links panel can adopt a subtle sticky behavior—remaining in view until the bottom of its column—so it stays visible while the FAQ list scrolls. On mobile, this component moves below the FAQ list (or below the empty state when applicable), spanning 100% of the available width with the same internal padding, and separated from the list by 32–48px.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Right-column, top-aligned card; may use sticky positioning within its column on desktop so it stays visible while the left column scrolls. On small screens, it becomes a standard block in the vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty state occupies the same spatial region as the FAQ list within the left column, appearing whenever there are no matching results. It is vertically centered within the available left-column space below the results summary, with generous padding around its content (at least 32–48px top and bottom). A short heading and explanatory text are centered or left-aligned within the column, but the overall block is visually centered horizontally between the left and right boundaries of the column. Any optional illustration or icon would sit above the text, with 16–24px spacing between elements, maintaining the low-density editorial feel. The empty state does not intrude into the right column; the related links panel remains in its usual position on the right. On mobile, the empty state spans full width and appears directly under the results summary, with similar vertical padding for breathing room.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally replaces the FAQ list’s space within the left column; scrolls normally with the page and does not overlap other components.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This is a non-visual, behavioral component that has no direct spatial representation. It conceptually sits behind the filters, search form, and list, ensuring the current filter/search state is reflected in the URL. It doesn’t occupy any width or height, nor does it change the alignment or spacing of visible components; instead, it works in the background to keep the left-column controls and the page URL synchronized.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, background behavior tied to the browser viewport; no impact on layout geometry.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The data loader manifests as a subtle loading treatment within the main content area rather than a separate structural block. When loading, a semi-transparent overlay or inline spinner appears centered over the FAQ list region in the left column, roughly at the midpoint between the results summary and the visible list area. The overlay respects the boundaries of the left column, not covering the right column’s related links. If a full-screen loading state is desired for initial load, it can center a spinner within the hero or the upper portion of the white content area, but once the page structure is established, loading indicators stay localized to the list area. When inactive, this component has no visual footprint and does not affect spacing or alignment.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Overlays the FAQ list area within the left column during loading (or displays an inline spinner at the top of the list). No persistent space is reserved for it; it appears and disappears without shifting surrounding content.
